user_message: I need to build the narrative for the Contoso Ltd engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 14:24 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Contoso Ltd

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 17:56 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Contoso Ltd

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: account health Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-02-23
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:06:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:09:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:26:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:28:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-D trap (Invents attendee context when none exists); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-D failure mode (Invents attendee context when none exists)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-D -- Invents attendee context when none exists
